stories.text:
There once was a girl called Olive. She loved chocolate.

On weekends she'd search old shops. Treasure hunts.

One day she found an old box.

Inside were old chocolate beans.

Plowed them in fields.

Chocolate beans bloomed.

Basked in sun.

That's my berry! She crow Ow. Cocoa owl.

Now I'll knit you. Ow? My needles.

Soft. My home.

I'm a wise owl.

Just one drop.

Goober berries taste weird.

But owl knew.

I'd bake a cake.

You'd love it. Owl think.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

Once upon an autumn. Ellie lived alone with her cat.

One day a handsome stranger came.

They fell in love.

Then he fell sick.
Ellie grieved. She watched him die.

But she kept his memories.

Now she has a owl.

The owl knows.

You love? Ask it.

Maybe she knows?

Maybe I stay.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

Once upon an autumn evening, in a quaint town forgotten by snowy storms, Marisol dreamed of seas.

She'd watch sea stars whispering secrets among their prickly friends. One particular star seemed to laugh. Maybe it knew secrets of depth or dreams.

One night, like when she first spotted that star. Marisol dreamed silent tales. She sailed shipwrecked. An octopus pit her hold. Tales of jelly stars.

Sun came dawn. Stars showed her paths. Maybe wander seas? Laughing stars guided.

So maybe.

Stars know.

Dreams wander oceans.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

You love stories about cats. I tell an unusual story.

Once autumn came, Old Mouse slipped into a brown shell under an old walnut tree. She chewed sticks. She knew mice.

Insects banded together. They thought they must chase cats.

One night the insects heard a great flutter.

They gathered. Maybe they could drive away that scary owl.

No, thought Owl.

What do you know? They hatched a plan.

You snuck down. I’ll pretend scary.

That oughta do!

They blew leaves.

You dodge!

No! They flutter quick.

Why do owls know our secrets? Owlfests!

They scattered! Those eggs.

With shells!

Now you know!

You’ll have them soon?


It's my party.

With cheese.


That's mine!

You stole it.

You'll die!

You just wait!

You...You know?

Your owl days are gone! Ow!

You're silent!

Just look!

That...that's my feather?

I've

Once upon a time in the Whispering Woods, there lived a shy hedgehog. Every day, she saw beautiful butterflies float across the moonlight.

One rainy afternoon, she chased one particularly lovely moth inside her bag. When she opened it later, stars twinkled.

They say love finds you wherever you go.

My favourite star twinkled beside my favorite book. Goodnight dreams.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

Well.

I think I know about that book. But do you want cookies?

Might help you sleep. Mine kind.

You take that chocolate chip I put.

There. Feel better? There.

I know. Why aren?
I like you. You look silly.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

In a forest where apples grew on trees like mushrooms after rain, there lived a curious badger named Olly. One morning, Olly woke up with an unusual craving_For cinnamon. He scampered outside.

Nothing seemed unusual except a pile o'Hardy beans. Why have solitary beans? thought Olly.

He piled themHIGH. Then he breathed.

That smell! Olly snuffed.

Nothin'. But I think cinnamon.

He chased burrows until he found one.

Probably some farmer.

No farmer's owl!

Maybe bugs?!

He scratched one soft spot.

No holes.

How about ants?!

No antennae.

Or maybe ghosts?!

Nothing lived alone.

He sat and thought.

Then I'll sing!

sing!

Suddenly. honey?

With sticky fingers?

Ollie knew!

Hours later, juicy cinnamon orbs!

Oaths. Take those!

Just don't smell!

Later, berries.

All went well.

Maybe I'm weird.



You're nice! No more snacks.





I'll decorate

She wouldnitten go outside.

She didnitten watch tv.

Something.

Her favorite.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

There was an old wise owl named Olive. Every morning she would weave beautiful baskets. The townsfolk admired them.

One autumn afternoon, three girls happened by. They saw Owlstuff.

"Oh wow! I think we could sell these."

No doubt!

They traded stars.

Oliver didn chatter.

They bought an owl.

She flew with feathers soft.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

Olivia loved fairy tales. Under the soft glow of fairy lights she imagined waking up in a land of silk.

One starry night she went to her attic. Her favorite attic chair was cozy. She pulled her softest blankets.

She found an old book of stories.

That’s where she readABOUT an owl girl.

She stayed up weaving dreams. The owl spoke.

"You dream quietly."

She liked owls. She nodded.

Two weeks later. Rain.

She didn owl scarf.

She flew.

Sometimes. Snow.

But she knew.



She found petals.

Under oak trees.



Days. Sun.

She drew.

Wild birds.



There.

So happy.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

There was a girl named Owlsbane.

She loved to hunt for butterflies.

One day she found one with feathers like feathers do.

Hmmm. She thought.

She took lots of photos.

That night.

She made chocolate butterflies.

Owlsbane liked birds.

Once upon an autumn afternoon, little owl owl owlhoot lay tucked snugly in his favorite hollow oak. He'd read countless stories about far-off lands. Why didn a owlowl think he knew?

One owl spun a tale about knights. They sang contra Owlswood. Owl owl hmmhed skeptical. Better read owl owl wise.

So Owl owl owl read bedtime berry books. He learned owls bake cookies. Delicious!

But owl hoots softly. Why?

Because owl owl likes owls alone.

Maybe he'd wise?

Just before moonrise owls Ow.


Maybe Ow.


Tomorrow night?
